title: Electricity generation from renewables
subtitle: Measured in [terawatt-hours](#dod:watt-hours). Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.
caption:
Electricity generation from renewables, 2025
Measured in terawatt-hours. Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.

This choropleth map displays the total electricity generated from renewable sources across countries in 2025, with values measured in terawatt-hours. The color scale ranges from pale green for the lowest generation levels to deep navy blue for the highest, with specific bands marking thresholds at 10, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1,000, and 2,000 TWh. The highest generation volumes appear in the darkest blue band, exceeding 1,000 TWh, visible in China and the United States. China shows the deepest shade on the map, placing it above the 2,000 TWh mark, while the United States falls in the band between 1,000 and 2,000 TWh. Brazil and Canada occupy the next tier, shaded in medium blue corresponding to the 500 to 1,000 TWh range. India and Russia appear in lighter blue tones, indicating generation between 200 and 500 TWh. Across Europe, Germany, Spain, France, and the United Kingdom show values in the 100 to 200 TWh band, while Nordic countries like Norway and Sweden fall in the 50 to 100 TWh range. Australia, Mexico, and Argentina display generation between 50 and 100 TWh. Most of Africa, the Middle East, and Central Asia appear in the palest green shades, indicating generation below 50 TWh, with many countries falling under the 10 TWh threshold. Greenland, Western Sahara, and several small island nations show no data, marked with diagonal hatching. The distribution shows a concentration of high renewable generation in a small number of large economies, with the majority of countries producing under 100 TWh.
